summary: Comprehensive testing of HFT production readiness improvements and mobile responsiveness completed. All 20 requested features verified working. Backend: 35/35 tests passed. Frontend: All mobile responsive features working correctly.

integration_issues.issue: Hydration warnings: <span> cannot be child of <tbody>, <tr>, <select>, <option> - existing issue from Emergent visual editor wrapper spans
integration_issues.note: This is a known Emergent platform issue with visual editor wrapper spans, not a code bug

passed_tests: GET /api/options/chain?underlying=NIFTY&num_strikes=5 returns live data with correct structure (spot_price, atm_strike, chain, totals)
GET /api/options/expiries?underlying=NIFTY returns sorted expiry dates
GET /api/options/underlyings returns available underlyings with indices first
GET /api/market/broker-status returns broker and market session info
GET /api/market/indices returns live index data
GET /api/arbitrage/cross-exchange returns cross-exchange arbitrage data
Option chain response caching works (2nd call within 2s returns faster)
GZip compression middleware is active (1297 bytes compressed vs 5644 bytes uncompressed = 77% reduction)
Mobile sidebar hamburger menu toggles sidebar open/close
Mobile header shows ARBITPRO logo and current page name
Sidebar overlay appears when sidebar is open on mobile
Dashboard header wraps properly on mobile (status badges, refresh btn)
Dashboard index cards display in 2-column grid on mobile
Option chain dropdowns (underlying, expiry, strikes) render inline on desktop
Option chain table has horizontal scroll on mobile (overflow-x: auto)
Option chain summary bar shows 3 cards on mobile, 5 on desktop
Auto-refresh toggle button works on option chain page
ATM strike row highlighted in yellow in option chain
Sidebar navigation works - click to navigate between pages
Broker status component visible in sidebar on both desktop and mobile
critical_code_review_comments: GZipMiddleware correctly configured with minimum_size=1000 for large responses
asyncio.to_thread used for blocking Angel One API calls - good HFT practice
2-second in-memory cache for option chain responses - appropriate for HFT polling
Indexed instrument master with O(1) lookups - good performance optimization
Mobile breakpoints at 1024px and 640px - standard responsive design
CSS transform used for mobile sidebar slide-in animation - smooth UX

success_rate.backend: 100% (35/35 tests passed - 21 option chain + 14 broker status)

test_credentials: No credentials required - public platform with Angel One broker pre-connected
context_for_next_testing_agent: Angel One broker is connected and market is OPEN (weekday). All HFT optimizations verified: GZip compression (77% reduction), asyncio.to_thread for blocking calls, 2s response caching, indexed instrument master. Mobile responsive features all working: hamburger menu, sidebar overlay, horizontal scroll tables, responsive grids. The hydration warnings are from Emergent visual editor wrapper spans, not code bugs.

Backend HFT Optimizations.asyncio.to_thread: PASS - Blocking API calls run in thread pool
Backend HFT Optimizations.Response Caching: PASS - 2s TTL cache for option chain

Option Chain API.GET /api/options/underlyings: PASS - Returns indices first, then stocks
Option Chain API.GET /api/options/chain: PASS - Returns spot_price, atm_strike, chain, totals

Market Data API.GET /api/market/broker-status: PASS - Returns broker + market session info

Mobile Responsive UI.ATM row highlight: PASS - Yellow highlight on ATM strike
Mobile Responsive UI.Broker status in sidebar: PASS - Visible on desktop and mobile
